originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Be Smart Online: Let's Talk About Videos and Being Safe!
content:
Hello, Super Kids of Africa!

Hello, wonderful kids of Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and today we're going to talk about something very important. It's about respecting each other and staying safe, especially when we use cameras and the internet. We hear stories sometimes, and it's always good to learn how to be smart, kind, and safe. These lessons help us protect ourselves and our friends.

What Happened Near Us?

Imagine you are walking around, playing with friends, or perhaps helping your family with chores. You are just having a normal day. Then, someone comes up to you with a camera or a phone. They start to record you. But here’s the important part: they don't ask you first if it's okay! How would that make you feel? Maybe a little surprised? A bit confused? Or even a little bit uncomfortable and sad? Well, something like this happened to some grown-up women in countries very close to us, like Kenya and Ghana. Kenya is a beautiful country known for its amazing wildlife, like giraffes and elephants, and vast open savannas! Ghana is famous for its lovely beaches, rich history, and warm, welcoming people. In these places, a man filmed some women without asking them for their permission. He just took videos of them when they were doing their everyday things. This is not a kind thing to do.

Why Asking for Permission is So Important

Think about it like sharing your favourite toy. If your friend wants to play with your special car or doll, they always ask you first, right? You can choose to say "yes, you can play" or "no, not right now." It's *your* toy, and you get to decide. It's the same with pictures and videos of you! Your face, your smile, your movements – they belong to *you*. No one should take a picture or a video of you without asking you first. This is a very important rule. We call this "getting permission" or "consent." It means someone agrees to something. **Did you know?** Just like you ask for permission to use someone's book, or to sit in someone's chair, people should always ask for permission before taking a picture or video of you! It’s the polite and respectful thing to do, and it shows you care about their feelings.

What Happens When Videos Go Online?

After people film things, sometimes they put these videos on the internet. The internet is like a huge, giant, magical library or a super big television screen where pictures, videos, and stories are shared. People from all over the world can see them! It’s a wonderful place to learn new things, watch fun cartoons, and even talk to family far away. But sometimes, people put videos or pictures on the internet without asking the people who are in them. And sometimes, other people who see these videos say not-so-nice things. This is called being "ridiculed" or "abused online." It means people use unkind, hurtful, or mean words about someone. This is never, ever okay. Everyone deserves to be treated with kindness and respect, whether they are online or offline. Imagine how sad you would feel if someone said unkind things about you in front of everyone. That's how it feels to be ridiculed online. **Did you know?** The internet connects people from every corner of the Earth! You can talk to someone in South Africa, Nigeria, Egypt, or even countries far away like India, Brazil, or the USA! It’s like having a window to the whole world!

Your Special Power: Saying "No!"

Remember that power we talked about? The power to say "yes" or "no" about your toys? You have that same special power when it comes to videos and pictures of you! Your body and your image are yours to protect. If someone points a camera at you and you don't want to be filmed, it is absolutely okay to say, "No, please don't film me." Or you can say, "Please ask my parent or guardian first." Your feelings are very important, and you have a right to decide if you want to be filmed or not. It’s like being a superhero for yourself! You can protect yourself by speaking up loudly and clearly. If you feel shy or scared to say "no" yourself, that's okay too. Just make sure you tell a trusted grown-up what happened as soon as you can. They will help you.

Being Kind to Everyone, Always

Imagine if someone said unkind things about your beautiful drawing or your favourite game. How would that make you feel? Not very happy, right? You would feel sad or angry. When videos are put online without asking, and then people say unkind things about the people in the video, it makes those people feel very sad, hurt, and angry. It's important to always remember that every single person deserves kindness and respect. We should always try our best to use kind words, whether we are talking to someone face-to-face in our village or typing a message on the internet from our home. **Did you know?** Being kind to others not only makes them feel good, but it makes *you* feel good too! It's like a happy boomerang – you send out kindness, and it comes right back to you! Kindness can make our communities and the whole world a happier place.

Who Can Help You Stay Safe?

You are never alone in this! There are many wonderful people around you who want to help you stay safe and happy. * **Your parents or guardians:** They love you more than anything and want to keep you safe from harm. Tell them everything. * **Your teachers:** They teach you important things at school, including how to be safe, both in real life and online. They are there to guide you. * **Other trusted adults:** This could be an auntie, uncle, grandmother, grandfather, an older sibling you trust, or a community leader. Think of an adult you feel safe talking to. If anyone tries to film you without asking, or if you ever see something online that makes you feel sad, scared, or uncomfortable, please tell a trusted adult right away. They will listen to you, understand what happened, and know what to do to help you. They can make sure you are protected. **Did you know?** Many schools in Africa are now teaching kids special lessons about how to be super safe online! They want to make sure you know all the rules to protect yourself in the digital world.

Tips for Being Super Online Smart!

Here are some simple but very important rules to help you be super smart and safe when you are online or using cameras: 1. **Always Ask First:** If *you* want to take a picture or video of someone, always, always ask them first! Wait for their "yes" before you press the button. It's the polite and right thing to do. 2. **Think Before You Share:** If you see something online, stop and think: Is it kind? Is it true? Is it helpful? If the answer is no to any of these, then don't share it. Let's spread happiness, not sadness. 3. **Don't Share Everything About Yourself:** It's good to keep some things private. Don't share your full name, where you live, what school you go to, or your phone number online without asking a grown-up first. This keeps you safe from strangers. 4. **Tell a Grown-Up If You're Uncomfortable:** If anything online makes you feel sad, scared, confused, or uncomfortable, tell a parent, teacher, or trusted adult right away. They can help you understand and fix the problem. 5. **Be a Good Online Friend:** If you see someone being unkind online, speak up (if it's safe to do so) or, even better, tell a grown-up. We should always stand up for kindness and help our friends.

Let's Be Safe and Kind Together!

So, my amazing friends, remember these important lessons. Everyone deserves respect. No one should film you without asking your permission. And if you ever feel uncomfortable or see something that's not right, please talk to a grown-up you trust. They are there for you. Let's all be kind, respectful, and super smart online. That way, our internet world can be a happy and safe place for everyone in Africa and beyond! Thank you for listening and for being such wonderful, smart kids!
summary: This news talks about how some grown-up women in Kenya and Ghana were filmed without being asked, and how these videos were shared online with unkind words, reminding kids that it's important to ask permission before filming anyone, to be kind online, and to tell a trusted adult if anything makes them feel uncomfortable.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone has a right to feel safe and respected, and no one should be filmed or have unkind things said about them online without their permission. Learning this helps kids protect themselves and be kind to others online.

originalTitle: South Africa's exclusion from G7 summit no surprise, says Ramaphosa
title: A Big Meeting Story: South Africa, Friends, and an Invitation!
content:
Hello there, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, ready to share a super interesting story with you. Today, we're talking about our wonderful home, Africa, and a special leader from South Africa! Get ready to learn some cool new things!

Meet Our President!

First, let's talk about our leaders! Every country has a leader who helps make big decisions and looks after all the people. In South Africa, our special leader is called President Ramaphosa. He is like the head teacher of the whole country! He works very hard to make South Africa a great place for everyone, especially for children like you. Did you know? South Africa is famous for its beautiful mountains, wide safaris with amazing animals like lions and elephants, and its many different cultures! That's why it's sometimes called the 'Rainbow Nation'!

What is the G7?

Now, imagine a special club. Not a club where you play games, but a club for some of the biggest and strongest countries in the world. This club is called the G7. 'G' stands for 'Group', and '7' means there are seven countries in this special club. These countries are like big friends who meet to talk about important things. They want to make the world a better place for everyone. The G7 countries are Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom (that's the UK), and the United States of America (that's the USA). Did you know? France is famous for its yummy pastries and a very tall tower called the Eiffel Tower! And Japan is known for its fast trains and beautiful cherry blossoms!

What is a 'Summit'?

When these G7 leaders want to meet and talk, they have something called a 'summit'. A summit is like a very, very important meeting. Imagine all your teachers having a big meeting to talk about how to make school even more fun for you! That's what a summit is, but for countries! At a summit, leaders sit down together. They share ideas. They talk about ways to help people and solve big problems around the world. It's a very big deal to be invited to a summit.

An Invitation for President Ramaphosa!

So, a while ago, one of the G7 countries, France, decided to host a big summit. They sent out invitations, just like when you send out invitations for your birthday party. And guess what? Our President, President Ramaphosa from South Africa, received an invitation! This was very exciting! It meant that the G7 countries wanted to hear what President Ramaphosa had to say. They wanted to include South Africa in their important talks. It showed that South Africa is a respected and important country in the world.

What Happened Next? A Little Problem!

But then, something a little tricky happened. Reports, which are like grown-up news stories, said that another big country, the United States, or USA, got involved. The leader of the USA at that time was President Trump. President Trump said he might not come to the big meeting if President Ramaphosa was there. This made France feel a little bit of *pressure*. Pressure is like when someone really, really wants you to do something, and you feel like you have to do it. Did you know? The USA is a very big country far away, known for its huge cities and places like Hollywood where movies are made! So, France made a difficult decision. They decided to *rescind* the invitation to President Ramaphosa. 'Rescind' is a big word, but it just means they took the invitation back. It's like if you invite a friend to play, but then something changes, and you have to say, "Sorry, maybe another time."

How Did President Ramaphosa Feel?

When President Ramaphosa heard this, he said it was "no surprise." What does that mean? It means he wasn't shocked or upset. He understood that sometimes in big world meetings, things can get complicated. He knew that countries sometimes have different ideas or special reasons for doing things. So, he wasn't angry. He just understood that sometimes, plans change. He is a very wise leader!

Why This is Okay for South Africa!

Even though our President didn't go to that particular G7 summit, it's really okay! South Africa is a strong and important country. We have many, many friends all over the world, not just in the G7 club. We work with other African countries. We work with countries in Asia, Europe, and many other places. South Africa is always busy making the world a better place, helping people, and growing stronger every day. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! We are all part of the big African family, and we work together to help each other! So, even if one invitation changed, it doesn't stop South Africa from being a wonderful, strong, and important country. President Ramaphosa continues to lead with wisdom and courage. We are proud of our country and our leaders! Keep shining bright, young Africans! You are the future, and we have so many exciting stories to share with you!
summary: South Africa's President Ramaphosa was invited to a big G7 meeting, but the invitation was taken back after the USA's President Trump shared his concerns, which President Ramaphosa understood and said was no surprise, showing South Africa remains strong and important.
whyMatters: This story helps us learn that even big leaders sometimes face changes in plans, but it doesn't stop our country, South Africa, from being important and having many friends around the world.

originalTitle: Norway to add more than $11bn to defence budget over 10 years
title: Super Safe Norway! A Faraway Country Gets Stronger!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter, here with some cool news from a place very far away. Imagine a land of icy mountains, beautiful lights in the sky, and even polar bears! This special place is called Norway. It's a country full of friendly people, just like us. Today, we are talking about how Norway wants to keep all its people super safe. Like when you have a strong lock on your door, or when your big brother or sister looks out for you. Countries also need ways to stay safe from anything bad.

What is 'Defence'?

Defence means keeping a country and its people safe. It’s like having a big shield to protect everyone. Countries have special people called soldiers. They are brave and train very hard. They learn how to keep peace and protect their land. Did you know? Soldiers also help people in times of trouble! If there's a big flood or something sad happens, soldiers can come and help rescue people and bring food. They are not just for fighting, but for helping too. Norway wants to make its shield even stronger. They want to make sure their soldiers have the best tools. These tools include special boats that sail on the sea, planes that fly high in the sky, and other important things. All these things help to keep the country safe.

More Money for Safety!

Imagine you have a piggy bank. You save money to buy something important, like a new toy or books for school. Countries also have 'money banks' and they use money for important things. Norway is going to put more money into its 'safety piggy bank'. They are planning to spend a lot of money – more than 11 billion dollars! Wow, that's a HUGE number! It's like having 11 thousand million one-dollar notes! This money will be spent over many years, about ten years. So, it's not all at once, but little by little. This big plan will help them buy newer and better things for their defence. It means more safety for all the people in Norway. Did you know? Money is not just for buying toys. Governments use money to build schools, hospitals, and roads! It also helps pay for teachers and doctors. So, money is super important for everyone.

A Club of Friends Called NATO

Norway is part of a very special club. It's like your group of best friends who always look out for each other. This club is called NATO. It stands for the North Atlantic Treaty Organization. That's a long name, right? NATO is a club of many countries from different parts of the world. They all promise to help each other if one of them ever needs protecting. It's like a big team that works together to keep everyone safe and peaceful. They want to make sure no one bothers their friends. Did you know? There are more than 30 countries in the NATO club! These countries come from places like Europe and North America. They all believe in peace and safety for their people.

The Big Friend, USA

One of the biggest and strongest friends in the NATO club is a country called the United States of America, or USA for short. You might have heard of it! USA wants all its friends in the NATO club to be strong too. They want every country to have a good defence. So, USA has been asking Norway and other countries in the club to spend more money on their safety. It's like a coach telling their team members to train hard so everyone is strong. This is not about being mean. It's about being prepared. If all the friends in the club are strong and ready, it helps keep the peace. When everyone is safe, we can all live happily.

What Does This Mean for Norway?

With more money, Norway can buy new ships for its navy. They can get better planes for their air force. And their brave soldiers will have even better equipment to do their job of keeping people safe. It’s all about protecting their beautiful country. This extra money will make Norway feel even more secure. It will help them protect their homes, their schools, and their special places like the fjords and snowy mountains. It means kids in Norway can play and learn feeling very safe. Did you know? Norway is famous for its long, narrow inlets of the sea between high cliffs, called fjords! They are beautiful natural wonders.

Why is This Important for Everyone?

Even though Norway is far away, what happens there can be important for the whole world. When countries are safe and peaceful, it helps make the whole world a safer place. When countries work together, like the NATO club, it shows that friendship and teamwork are very strong. It teaches us that helping each other is a good thing. We all want a world where everyone can live in peace and safety, right? So, Norway's plan to boost its safety is good news. It shows they care deeply about their people and want to make sure everyone is protected. And that's something to cheer about! Stay safe and keep shining, my wonderful friends! See you next time with more cool news!
summary: Norway, a country far away, is planning to spend a lot more money over the next ten years to make sure its people and country are super safe, just like its friends in the NATO club want them to be.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries like Norway are strong and safe, it helps make the whole world a more peaceful place for everyone, including kids like you.

originalTitle: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
title: Sad News from a Faraway City: Homes and Hearts in Tehran
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter from Africa here, bringing you important stories from around our big, beautiful world. Today, we have some news from a place very far away from us, a big city called Tehran. Tehran is the capital city of a country named Iran. It’s a city where many, many people live, just like in some of our biggest cities here in Africa!

Big, Sad Noises in Tehran

Imagine your home. It’s a special place, right? It’s where you feel safe, where you eat yummy food, play with your toys, and sleep soundly at night. Well, in Tehran, something very, very sad has been happening. For about one whole month, there have been big, loud noises in the city. These noises were so strong and so loud that they made many houses and other buildings break. Many homes, where families once lived happily, are now broken and unsafe.

Our Homes Keep Us Safe

Our homes are like big, warm hugs that keep us safe from the rain, the hot sun, and anything else outside. They are where our families gather, share stories, and make happy memories. When homes break, it’s not just bricks and wood that are damaged; people feel very, very sad and scared inside. It’s hard to play or even sleep when your special safe place isn't safe anymore.

What is 'Rubble'?

When buildings break into many, many tiny pieces, or even big pieces, we call all those broken bits 'rubble'. Imagine a giant pile of broken toys, but instead, it’s broken bricks, wood, metal, and glass. It's very messy and it can be dangerous to walk around. People have to carefully look through this rubble to try and find their special things, like old photos or maybe even their family members who might have been inside when the breaking happened.

Sad Stories from Families

Many, many people in Tehran are feeling very, very sad right now. They told a news group called the BBC that their lives have changed so much, and it's very hard. One mom shared a heartbreaking story that made many people sad. She said, “My daughter is under the rubble.” This means her little girl was somewhere among the broken pieces of their home. Can you imagine how scared and sad that mom must be, just wanting to find her child and make sure she is safe? Her heart must be very sore. **Did you know?** Tehran is a very old city, with a history going back thousands of years! It's also one of the biggest cities in the world, with millions of people living there, just like Lagos or Cairo!

Where Do Families Go?

When homes are broken, families suddenly don't have a place to live. They might have to sleep outside in tents, or in big community shelters where many families stay together. Sometimes, if they are lucky, they can go and stay with friends or other family members. But it’s not easy. It means no comfy bed, no private space, and maybe even losing all your favorite toys and books. For children, not having a home means not having their own space to play and learn.

Helping Hands and Kind Hearts

Even when things are very sad and hard, people always try to help each other. Neighbors help neighbors find things or share what little food they have. Brave people come to help clear the rubble and look for others. People share blankets and warm clothes. It’s so important to be kind and help others when they are going through such a difficult time. Just like we help our friends if they fall or need a hug, people in Tehran are helping each other. **Did you know?** The name “Tehran” means “bottom of the mountain” because the city is built at the foot of big, beautiful mountains! Imagine playing with snow on mountains close to a big city!

Why Did These Sad Things Happen?

Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries have big disagreements. These disagreements can get very, very serious. Sadly, sometimes these serious disagreements lead to loud noises and breaking houses, like what happened in Tehran. Two countries, the United States and Israel, were involved. But what’s most important for all of us to remember is that it’s the ordinary people living in Tehran – the moms, the dads, and especially the children – who feel the most sadness and who have lost their homes and their sense of safety.

Wishing for Peace for Everyone

We all want to live in a world where everyone is safe and happy in their homes. We wish for peace for all the people in Tehran, and for people everywhere else in the world. Peace means no loud noises, no broken homes, and no sad hearts. It means everyone can go to school, play with their friends, and be with their family without any fear. **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa work very hard to promote peace and help people who are in need all over the world. We believe that we are all one big global family, and we should always help each other!

What Can We Learn from This?

This sad news from Tehran helps us to remember how lucky we are to have safe homes and our families around us. It also teaches us to be extra kind and understanding to everyone, no matter where they live or what they look like. When we hear about sad things happening far away, it makes us think about how we can make our own neighborhoods and our own world a kinder, safer, and more peaceful place for every child and every grown-up. **Did you know?** Children all over the world love to play the same kinds of games! They love to run, jump, draw, and tell stories. Children in Tehran also loved to play in parks and with their friends before these sad things happened. They deserve to be happy and safe, just like you.

Our Hopes for the Future

We hope with all our hearts that the people in Tehran can find their loved ones, rebuild their homes, and start to feel safe and happy again very, very soon. We send them our warmest wishes and our thoughts of peace from here in Africa. Let's all hope for a world full of peace, kindness, and safety for every child and every family, everywhere.
